title: Unit 17 Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass: Adverbs & Expressions of Manner

script.text: Welcome... to the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 17: Adverbs. In Unit 6... we learned adjectives. They describe 'Who'. Today... we learn adverbs. They describe 'How'. 'Quickly'... 'Slowly'... 'Well'. I'm William... and here with me is Athina. Athina... how do we build an adverb?
Γεια σας!
It is the easiest rule in Greek! Just take the adjective and change the ending to an 'a'.
Καλός – καλά. Γρήγορος – γρήγορα. Ωραία.
Well... quickly... beautifully. They never change! Adverbs are indeclinable. They are the same for everyone.
And don't forget the adverbs of time and place. The bricks of conversation.
Εδώ, εκεί. Σήμερα, αύριο, χθες.
Here, there. Today, tomorrow, yesterday.
Now... we have a very important stress test. Athina, explain the difference between 'when' and 'never'.
Listen closely to the accent.
Πότε;
With the stress at the start... means 'When?'. But...
Ποτέ.
With the stress at the end... means 'Never'.
One little accent mark changes a question into a lifetime commitment! And speaking of 'never'... Greek uses double negatives, right?
Yes! In English, you say 'I don't drink anything'. In Greek, we say 'I don't drink nothing'!
Δεν πίνω ποτέ. Δεν θέλω καθόλου.
I don't drink never. I don't want not at all. Two negatives make a very strong Greek 'No'!

Double the negative... double the power! This unit has provided you with essential syntactic anchors. You now have the 'How', the 'When', and the 'Where'. Excellent work... we'll see you in the next unit.
Γεια σας!
